blocks.text: Server-Sent Events (SSE) let the server push updates to the client over a single long-lived HTTP connection. Unlike WebSockets, SSE is unidirectional (server → client) and uses regular HTTP — great for live feeds, notifications, and progress updates.
ctx.sse() handles the SSE framing, headers (Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache), and keep-alive automatically.

await send({ data: { user: "leo" }, event: "join", id: 1 });
await send({ data: { message: "hello" }, event: "chat", id: 2, retry: 3000 });
In a browser island, use the native EventSource API to consume SSE streams.

items: Live notifications — push alerts to connected users without polling
Progress bars — stream progress of long-running server operations
Chat feeds — append messages to a live chat UI
Dashboard metrics — push updated stats every few seconds
AI streaming — stream LLM token output progressively
